title: schoenus evansianus
intro: Schoenus evansianus, also known as Evans' sedge, is a species of plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The leaves and roots of Schoenus evansianus are believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promote overall health and well-being.
